https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pul: Lithuanian account translation is wrong-language (Italian) after change: themes/src/main/resources-community/theme/base/account/messages/messages_lt.properties sets `totpStep1=Installa una delle seguenti applicazioni sul tuo cellulare:` which is not Lithuanian.

Lithuanian login translation is wrong-language (Italian) after change: themes/src/main/resources-community/theme/base/login/messages/messages_lt.properties sets `loginTotpStep1=Installa una delle seguenti applicazioni sul tuo cellulare:` which is not Lithuanian.

Simplified Chinese (zh_CN) resource now contains Traditional Chinese wording/characters: themes/src/main/resources-community/theme/base/account/messages/messages_zh_CN.properties changes `totpStep1` to `在您的手機上安裝以下應用程式之一：` (uses 手機/應用程式), inconsistent for zh_CN.

verifySafeHtml() derives the English file path with `replaceAll("_[a-zA-Z-_]*\\.properties", "_en.properties")`, which will also match `_en.properties` and rewrite it to `_en.properties` again, producing an invalid path like `..._en_en.properties` when verifying an English file; this would cause FileNotFound and the method throws RuntimeException. Evidence: VerifyMessageProperties.java `String englishFile = ...replaceAll("_[a-zA-Z-_]*\\.properties", "_en.properties");` followed by `new FileInputStream(englishFile)` with `throw new RuntimeException(...)` on failure.

verifySafeHtml() throws unchecked RuntimeException on IO failures (both for the target bundle and the derived English bundle) instead of MojoExecutionException like verify() does, causing inconsistent/incorrect plugin failure behavior. Evidence: VerifyMessageProperties.java catches IOException in verify() and throws MojoExecutionException, but verifySafeHtml() catches IOException and throws RuntimeException for both file reads.

AccessTokenContext constructor has a copy/paste null-check bug: it validates grantType twice and never validates rawTokenId. Evidence in services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/protocol/oidc/encode/AccessTokenContext.java: `Objects.requireNonNull(grantType, "Null grantType not allowed");` followed by `Objects.requireNonNull(grantType, "Null rawTokenId not allowed");` (should be `rawTokenId`). This allows `rawTokenId` to be null without failing fast.

AssertEvents.isAccessTokenId() matcher has inverted grant-shortcut comparison logic, rejecting correct shortcuts and accepting incorrect ones. Evidence in testsuite/.../AssertEvents.java: `if (items[0].substring(3, 5).equals(expectedGrantShortcut)) return false;` then `return isUUID().matches(items[1]);` — it returns false when the shortcut matches.

AssertEvents.isAccessTokenId() extracts the grant shortcut using wrong substring indices. The encoded context is 6 chars: sessionType(2)+tokenType(2)+grantType(2), so grant shortcut is at indices [4,6), but code uses `substring(3, 5)`. Evidence in testsuite/.../AssertEvents.java: comment says grant shortcut starts at 4th char, but code uses `items[0].substring(3, 5)`.

CredentialHelper.createRecoveryCodesCredential hard-codes provider id "keycloak-recovery-authn-codes" and does not null-check the returned provider. If the id is wrong or provider not registered, recoveryCodeCredentialProvider will be null and the fallback path will throw NPE when calling createCredential(...). Evidence: `var recoveryCodeCredentialProvider = session.getProvider(CredentialProvider.class, "keycloak-recovery-authn-codes"); ... recoveryCodeCredentialProvider.createCredential(realm, user, credentialModel);`

CredentialHelper.createRecoveryCodesCredential fallback stores the wrong data: it serializes generatedCodes into a UserCredentialModel and calls user.credentialManager().updateCredential(recoveryCodesCredential), but if that returns false it falls back to `recoveryCodeCredentialProvider.createCredential(realm, user, credentialModel)` where `credentialModel` does not include the generatedCodes JSON. This can result in creating a local DB recovery-codes credential without the generated codes. Evidence: `recoveryCodesJson = JsonSerialization.writeValueAsString(generatedCodes); ... UserCredentialModel recoveryCodesCredential = new UserCredentialModel("", credentialModel.getType(), recoveryCodesJson); ... if (!userStorageCreated) { recoveryCodeCredentialProvider.createCredential(realm, user, credentialModel); }`

RecoveryAuthnCodeInputLoginBean can throw NoSuchElementException: it now uses `RecoveryAuthnCodesUtils.getCredential(user)` (which may return Optional.empty()) but still unconditionally calls `.get()` on the Optional. Evidence: `Optional<CredentialModel> credentialModelOpt = RecoveryAuthnCodesUtils.getCredential(user); ... RecoveryAuthnCodesCredentialModel.createFromCredentialModel(credentialModelOpt.get());`

Docs/exit-code mapping mismatch: CompatibilityResult changes RECREATE_UPGRADE_EXIT_CODE from 4 to 3 and introduces FEATURE_DISABLED=4 (quarkus/runtime/src/main/java/org/keycloak/quarkus/runtime/compatibility/CompatibilityResult.java), but docs only show an entry for exit code 4 (feature disabled) and do not document the new recreate-required exit code 3 (docs/guides/server/update-compatibility.adoc). This makes the published exit-code contract incomplete/incorrect for users/scripts relying on documented exit codes.

ClientPermissionsV2.hasPermission(String scope) looks up the global/all-clients resource by name using the literal resource type string ("Clients") instead of using the schema helper used elsewhere (AdminPermissionsSchema.SCHEMA.getResourceTypeResource(...)). In the same class, per-client fallback uses getResourceTypeResource(session, server, CLIENTS_RESOURCE_TYPE), but the global check uses resourceStore.findByName(server, AdminPermissionsSchema.CLIENTS_RESOURCE_TYPE, server.getId()). This inconsistency can cause global client permissions (e.g., MANAGE/VIEW for all clients) to never be found/evaluated, resulting in false-deny behavior. Evidence: ClientPermissionsV2#hasPermission(String) vs ClientPermissionsV2#hasPermission(ClientModel, ...).

PermissionClientTest.onAfter() can throw NullPointerException because it assumes permissions.findAll(...) is non-null and immediately calls .forEach(...). In this same test suite, UserResourceTypePermissionTest.testDelete asserts that getScopePermissionsResource(client).findAll(...) can return null (assertThat(existing, nullValue())), so the cleanup in PermissionClientTest is unsafe and can NPE. Evidence: PermissionClientTest.onAfter(): permissions.findAll(...).forEach(...); and UserResourceTypePermissionTest.testDelete(): assertThat(existing, nullValue()).

PermissionClientTest.testManageOnlyOneClient unconditionally accesses default client scopes at index 1 (get(1)) twice. If the client has fewer than 2 default client scopes, this will throw IndexOutOfBoundsException and fail the test. Evidence: PermissionClientTest.testManageOnlyOneClient: clientResource.getDefaultClientScopes().get(1) and later defaultClientScopes.get(1).

PermissionClientTest.testConfigureOnlyOneClient unconditionally accesses defaultClientScopes.get(0) when asserting forbidden operations. If the default client scopes list is empty, this will throw IndexOutOfBoundsException and fail the test. Evidence: PermissionClientTest.testConfigureOnlyOneClient: defaultClientScopes.get(0) used in removeDefaultClientScope(...) and addDefaultClientScope(...).

GroupPermissionsV2.canManage() treats VIEW as sufficient for manage: it returns hasPermission(null, AdminPermissionsSchema.VIEW, AdminPermissionsSchema.MANAGE), so a caller with only VIEW on groups will be considered able to manage groups. Evidence: GroupPermissionsV2.java `public boolean canManage() { ... return hasPermission(null, AdminPermissionsSchema.VIEW, AdminPermissionsSchema.MANAGE); }`

GroupPermissionsV2.getGroupIdsWithViewPermission() returns authorization Resource IDs, not Group IDs, and callers store these into UserModel.GROUPS expecting group IDs. It iterates resources and adds `groupResource.getId()` to the returned set, and also passes that value into hasPermission which looks up by name. Evidence: GroupPermissionsV2.java `granted.add(groupResource.getId());` and UsersResource/BruteForceUsersResource `session.setAttribute(UserModel.GROUPS, groupIds);`

GroupPermissionsV2.getGroupIdsWithViewPermission()/hasPermission() mixes up resource id vs resource name: it passes `groupResource.getId()` into hasPermission, but hasPermission does `resourceStore.findByName(server, groupId)`. If resource id != resource name, lookup fails and permission evaluation falls back to the type resource, producing incorrect results. Evidence: GroupPermissionsV2.java `if (hasPermission(groupResource.getId(), ...))` and `Resource resource = ... resourceStore.findByName(server, groupId);`

ApiUtil.handleCreatedResponse now closes the Response via try-with-resources, but new test code also wraps the same Response in try-with-resources and calls handleCreatedResponse inside, causing a double-close of the same Response object. Evidence: ApiUtil.java `try (response) { ... }` and GroupResourceTypeEvaluationTest.java `try (Response response = ... ) { ... ApiUtil.handleCreatedResponse(response); ... }`

AuthzClientCryptoProvider.getBouncyCastleProvider() returns the provider of the default KeyStore type (e.g., SUN), not a BouncyCastle/BCFIPS provider. Evidence: `return KeyStore.getInstance(KeyStore.getDefaultType()).getProvider();` in `AuthzClientCryptoProvider#getBouncyCastleProvider()`. This is an incorrect mapping for a method explicitly named/contracted to return the BouncyCastle provider and can break callers expecting BC-specific behavior/algorithms.

Dead/copy-paste code in `concatenatedRSToASN1DER()`: two ASN1Encoder instances are created and written to but their outputs are discarded, indicating an implementation mistake. Evidence in `AuthzClientCryptoProvider#getEcdsaCryptoProvider().concatenatedRSToASN1DER`: `ASN1Encoder.create().write(rBigInteger); ASN1Encoder.create().write(sBigInteger);` followed by constructing a different encoder for the returned sequence.

GroupAdapter.getSubGroupsCount() can now return null when modelSupplier.get() returns null: `GroupModel model = modelSupplier.get(); return model == null ? null : model.getSubGroupsCount();`. This is a behavioral/API contract change from always delegating to the underlying model and can introduce new NullPointerExceptions in callers that unbox or assume a non-null count.

New test createMultiDeleteMultiReadMulti() starts a background reader thread but never joins it (thread reference is not kept). The main thread sets `deletedAll` and immediately asserts `caughtExceptions` is empty, so exceptions can be added after the assertion and the test becomes racy/flaky. Evidence: `new Thread(...).start(); ... deletedAll.set(true); assertThat(caughtExceptions, Matchers.empty());`.
https://github.com/ai-code-review-evalua: Test logic bug in PasskeysUsernamePasswordFormTest.reauthenticationOfUserWithoutPasskey(): it asserts driver.findElement(...) returns null, but Selenium findElement never returns null (it returns a WebElement or throws NoSuchElementException). The code then calls fail() if no exception is thrown, so the nullValue() assertion is meaningless/unreachable when the element is absent and the test relies on catching a generic Exception. Evidence: try { MatcherAssert.assertThat(driver.findElement(By.xpath("//form[@id='webauth']")), nullValue()); fail("Not expected to have webauthn button"); } catch (Exception nsee) { // expected } (appears twice).
https://github.com/getsentry/sentry/pull: Malformed Click option declaration for flusher processes: click.Option is given param_decls ["--flusher-processes", "flusher_processes"], but the second entry is not a valid option flag (missing leading dashes). This will be treated as another option declaration and can break CLI option registration/parsing. Evidence: src/sentry/consumers/__init__.py: click.Option(["--flusher-processes", "flusher_processes"], default=1, type=int, ...).

Inconsistent metric tag key for the same shard dimension: one timer uses tags={"shard": shard_tag} while another uses tags={"shards": shard_tag}, splitting metrics into different series and likely breaking dashboards/aggregation. Evidence: src/sentry/spans/consumers/process/flusher.py: metrics.timer("spans.buffer.flusher.produce", tags={"shard": shard_tag}) vs metrics.timer("spans.buffer.flusher.wait_produce", tags={"shards": shard_tag}).

SpanFlusher can crash with ZeroDivisionError when max_processes is set to 0: num_processes becomes 0 (min(0, len(assigned_shards)) == 0) and then process_index = i % self.num_processes divides by zero. Evidence: src/sentry/spans/consumers/process/flusher.py: self.num_processes = min(self.max_processes, len(buffer.assigned_shards)); for i, shard in enumerate(buffer.assigned_shards): process_index = i % self.num_processes.

SpanFlusher._ensure_processes_alive mutates self.processes while iterating it, which can raise RuntimeError ('dictionary changed size during iteration') during restarts. Evidence: for process_index, process in self.processes.items(): ... self._create_process_for_shards(process_index, shards) ... and _create_process_for_shards assigns self.processes[process_index] = process.
https://github.com/ai-code-review-evalua: WidgetCardChart feature-flagged path renders TableWidgetVisualization with hardcoded empty `columns` and empty `tableData` instead of using `result.data`/`result.meta`, so tables will always appear empty when `organization.features.includes('use-table-widget-visualization')` is true. Evidence: in `static/app/views/dashboards/widgetCard/chart.tsx`, the flagged branch passes `columns={[]}` and `tableData={{data: [], meta: {fields: {}, units: {}}}}`.

TableWidgetVisualization can throw at runtime when `columns` is not provided because it calls `Object.keys(tableData?.meta.fields)`; optional chaining only guards `tableData`, not `meta`, so if `tableData.meta` is undefined this becomes `Object.keys(undefined)` and crashes. Evidence: `static/app/views/dashboards/widgets/tableWidget/tableWidgetVisualization.tsx` uses `Object.keys(tableData?.meta.fields).map(...)`.

useFeedbackSummary can crash when the query is disabled (enabled=false): after `useApiQuery`, if `isPending` and `isError` are both false, it unconditionally reads `data.summary`, `data.numFeedbacksUsed`, and `data.success` even though `data` can be undefined when the query never ran. Evidence: `static/app/components/feedback/list/useFeedbackSummary.tsx` returns `{ summary: data.summary, tooFewFeedbacks: data.numFeedbacksUsed === 0 && !data.success }` without guarding `data`.

organization_auditlogs.py references `organization_context` but it is not defined in the shown scope, so evaluating `enable_advanced = request.user.is_superuser or organization_context.member.has_global_access` will raise NameError at runtime.

OptimizedCursorPaginator.get_item_key uses `math.floor/ceil` on `value = getattr(item, self.key)`. In this endpoint the paginator is used with `order_by='-datetime'`, so the key will be a datetime; passing a datetime to math.floor/ceil will raise TypeError at runtime.

OptimizedCursorPaginator.get_item_key references `math` but the diff does not show `math` being imported in paginator.py; this will raise NameError when get_item_key is called (unless math is imported elsewhere in the file, which is not shown here).

OptimizedCursorPaginator.get_item_key calls `self._is_asc(for_prev)`, but the diff does not show `_is_asc` being defined on OptimizedCursorPaginator; if BasePaginator does not provide `_is_asc`, this will raise AttributeError at runtime.

OptimizedCursorPaginator explicitly allows negative queryset slicing when `enable_advanced_features` is true and `cursor.offset < 0` (`results = list(queryset[start_offset:stop])` with start_offset negative). Django QuerySet slicing does not support negative indices, so this will raise at runtime when a negative offset cursor is provided.
https://github.com/grafana/grafana/pull/: Compile-time error: NewResourceServer calls s.Init(ctx) but no ctx is defined in the shown function scope. Diff shows `err := s.Init(ctx)` added without introducing/deriving `ctx`, which would be `undefined: ctx` unless it already exists outside the shown context.
https://github.com/getsentry/sentry/pull: MetricAlertDetectorHandler now subclasses StatefulDetectorHandler but provides no implementations for its abstract methods (get_dedupe_value, get_group_key_values, build_occurrence_and_event_data). In src/sentry/incidents/grouptype.py it is defined as `class MetricAlertDetectorHandler(StatefulDetectorHandler[QuerySubscriptionUpdate]): pass`, which makes it abstract/non-instantiable and will raise TypeError if used.

Test bug: In TestProcessDetectors.test_state_results_multi_group, the second expected occurrence is built with the wrong value. Input has `group_vals`: {"group_1": 6, "group_2": 10} but the test calls `build_mock_occurrence_and_event(..., "group_2", 6, PriorityLevel.HIGH)` instead of using 10, masking value propagation issues.

Test bug: Multiple tests build expected occurrences/event_data using a hardcoded value of 6 even when the evaluated packet value differs (e.g. 100, 8). Examples: `handler.evaluate(...{"val1": 100})` then `build_mock_occurrence_and_event(handler, "val1", 6, ...)`, and similarly for value 8. This makes expectations inconsistent with inputs and can hide incorrect wiring of `value` into occurrence/event_data.

get_monitor_environment_context computes a modified `config` copy (humanized `schedule_type`) but returns the original unmodified `monitor_environment.monitor.config`, so the transformation is a no-op and emitted context data is incorrect. Evidence (incident_occurrence.py):
```py
config = monitor_environment.monitor.config.copy()
if "schedule_type" in config:
    config["schedule_type"] = monitor_environment.monitor.get_schedule_type_display()
...
return {
    ...
    "config": monitor_environment.monitor.config,
    ...
}
```

sentry/monitors/types.py adds `class SimpleCheckIn(TypedDict):` but the diff does not show `TypedDict` being imported in that file; this will raise `NameError` at import time if not already imported earlier in the file. Evidence (types.py):
```py
class SimpleCheckIn(TypedDict):
    ...
```

AssignmentSource.queued uses a dynamic default incorrectly: `queued: datetime = timezone.now()` is evaluated at class definition/import time, so all instances created without an explicit `queued` will share the same timestamp. In a dataclass this should use `field(default_factory=timezone.now)`. Evidence: `src/sentry/integrations/services/assignment_source.py` defines `queued: datetime = timezone.now()`.

Cycle-prevention ID mismatch: `AssignmentSource.from_integration` stores `integration_id=integration.id`, but `IssueSyncIntegration.should_sync` compares `sync_source.integration_id` against `self.org_integration.integration_id`. These are different ID sources (Integration.id vs OrganizationIntegration.integration_id), so the equality check can fail and not prevent sync cycles. Evidence: `AssignmentSource.from_integration(... integration_id=integration.id)` and `if sync_source and sync_source.integration_id == self.org_integration.integration_id: return False` in `src/sentry/integrations/mixins/issues.py`.

Uses non-existent stdlib exception `queue.ShutDown` in `OrderedQueueWorker.run()`. The diff shows `except queue.ShutDown:` in two places, but `queue` is imported from the Python standard library (`import queue`), where `ShutDown` is not available in supported versions. This will raise `AttributeError` when evaluating the except clause and breaks shutdown handling.
Evidence: `src/sentry/remote_subscriptions/consumers/queue_consumer.py`:
- `import queue`
- `except queue.ShutDown:` (twice in `OrderedQueueWorker.run`).

Calls non-existent method `shutdown()` on `queue.Queue` instances. The diff constructs queues via `queue.Queue()` and later calls `q.shutdown(immediate=False)` in `FixedQueuePool.shutdown()`, but stdlib `queue.Queue` has no `shutdown` method in supported versions. This will raise `AttributeError` (caught and logged), leaving worker threads potentially blocked on `get()`.
Evidence: `src/sentry/remote_subscriptions/consumers/queue_consumer.py`:
- `work_queue: queue.Queue[WorkItem[T]] = queue.Queue()`
- `q.shutdown(immediate=False)` in `FixedQueuePool.shutdown()`.

Shutdown can hang/leak threads: workers block on `work_queue.get()` and are only unblocked via the (invalid) `q.shutdown()`/`queue.ShutDown` mechanism. Setting `worker.shutdown = True` does not interrupt a blocking `get()`, so `worker.join(timeout=5.0)` may time out and threads keep running.
Evidence: `src/sentry/remote_subscriptions/consumers/queue_consumer.py`:
- `work_item = self.work_queue.get()` in `OrderedQueueWorker.run()`
- `FixedQueuePool.shutdown()` sets `worker.shutdown = True` then calls `q.shutdown(...)` (invalid) and finally `worker.join(timeout=5.0)`.

Offsets are marked complete even when processing fails, contradicting the strategy docstring claim "Only commits offsets after successful processing" and causing at-most-once behavior/data loss on exceptions. `OrderedQueueWorker.run()` catches `Exception` from `result_processor` but still calls `offset_tracker.complete_offset(...)` in `finally`, enabling commits for failed messages.
Evidence: `src/sentry/remote_subscriptions/consumers/queue_consumer.py`:
- Docstring in `SimpleQueueProcessingStrategy`: "Only commits offsets after successful processing"
- `OrderedQueueWorker.run()`: `except Exception: ...` then `finally: self.offset_tracker.complete_offset(...)`.

OffsetTracker state leak/inconsistency: `mark_committed()` removes committed offsets from `all_offsets` but does not remove them from `outstanding`, leaving stale offsets in memory indefinitely.
Evidence: `src/sentry/remote_subscriptions/consumers/queue_consumer.py`:
- `mark_committed()` updates `last_committed` and filters `self.all_offsets[partition]` but never cleans `self.outstanding[partition]`.

New uptime tests reference `kafka_definition` without importing it, causing `NameError`. The diff adds multiple uses of `kafka_definition.get_topic_codec(...)` in `tests/sentry/uptime/consumers/test_results_consumer.py` but does not add an import for `kafka_definition` in that file.
Evidence: `tests/sentry/uptime/consumers/test_results_consumer.py`:
- `codec = kafka_definition.get_topic_codec(kafka_definition.Topic.UPTIME_RESULTS)` (multiple occurrences)
- No corresponding `import kafka_definition` added in the shown imports.

New uptime test uses `call(...)` without importing it, causing `NameError`. The diff shows `mock_processor_call.assert_has_calls([call(...), ...])` but only imports `from unittest import mock`.
Evidence: `tests/sentry/uptime/consumers/test_results_consumer.py`:
- `mock_processor_call.assert_has_calls([call("uptime", result_1), call("uptime", result_2)], any_order=True)`
- No `from unittest.mock import call` and no `call = mock.call`.
https://github.com/ai-code-review-evalua: OptimizedCursorPaginator.get_item_key uses math.floor/math.ceil but paginator.py does not import math, causing NameError when the paginator is used. Evidence: src/sentry/api/paginator.py new class OptimizedCursorPaginator calls math.floor/ceil with no added import in diff.

OptimizedCursorPaginator.get_item_key assumes the cursor key is numeric and applies math.floor/ceil; organization audit logs paginate with order_by='-datetime' so the key will be a datetime, causing TypeError and breaking the optimized audit log pagination path. Evidence: src/sentry/api/endpoints/organization_auditlogs.py uses paginator_cls=OptimizedCursorPaginator with order_by='-datetime'; src/sentry/api/paginator.py get_item_key does math.floor/ceil on getattr(item, self.key).

OptimizedCursorPaginator.get_result can slice a Django QuerySet with a negative start index when enable_advanced_features is true and cursor.offset < 0, which Django QuerySet slicing does not support (raises 'Negative indexing is not supported'), causing runtime errors. Evidence: src/sentry/api/paginator.py: if self.enable_advanced_features and cursor.offset < 0: start_offset = cursor.offset; results = list(queryset[start_offset:stop]).

BasePaginator.get_result now allows negative start offsets when cursor.is_prev is true (start_offset = offset), which can lead to negative QuerySet slicing and runtime errors if offset is negative. Evidence: src/sentry/api/paginator.py: start_offset = max(0, offset) if not cursor.is_prev else offset; results = list(queryset[start_offset:stop]).

Paginator trimming logic uses the original offset in a length comparison even though slicing may use start_offset, which can be different (e.g., clamped to 0). This can cause incorrect trimming (dropping/keeping wrong items) and cursor construction. Evidence: src/sentry/api/paginator.py: slice uses start_offset, but later condition is `elif len(results) == offset + limit + extra:`.

Span consumer now unconditionally reads val['end_timestamp_precise']; if the field is missing in incoming payloads, this will raise KeyError and break span processing. Evidence: src/sentry/spans/consumers/process/factory.py: end_timestamp_precise=val["end_timestamp_precise"].

SpansBuffer._load_segment_data removed the max_segment_spans enforcement entirely, so segments with excessive span counts will no longer be skipped, potentially causing memory/processing blowups compared to the previous safety behavior. Evidence: src/sentry/spans/buffer.py diff removes the block that checked len(payloads[key]) > self.max_segment_spans and deleted payloads/cursors.

tests/snuba/api/endpoints/test_organization_events_stats.py uses @mock.patch(...) but there is no import for mock in the shown diff, which will raise NameError during test collection/execution. Evidence: decorators `@mock.patch("sentry.api.helpers.error_upsampling.options")` were added, but no corresponding `import mock` / `from unittest import mock` was added in this file segment.

tests/snuba/api/endpoints/test_organization_events_stats.py setUp overwrites self.user after calling self.login_as(user=self.user), so the authenticated user differs from the later self.user used in the test setup, making the test state inconsistent and potentially breaking auth-dependent behavior. Evidence: `self.login_as(user=self.user)` then later `self.user = self.create_user()`.
https://github.com/grafana/grafana/pull/: TestService_CacheCheck("Should deny on explicit cache deny entry") has an internally inconsistent setup: the comment says it is allowing access via permCache, but it actually stores a false value. Evidence: `// Allow access to the dashboard to prove this is not checked` followed by `s.permCache.Set(..., map[string]bool{"dashboards:uid:dash1": false})`. This can mask whether the deny-cache truly overrides an allow.
https://github.com/getsentry/sentry/pull: OAuthLoginView can raise a 500 on network/HTTP errors during token exchange because `safe_urlopen(...)` is called outside the try/except. Evidence: `req = safe_urlopen(url=ghip.get_oauth_access_token_url(), data=data)` is not wrapped, while only `safe_urlread(req)` parsing is inside `try:`; any exception from `safe_urlopen` will bubble up instead of returning the integration-failed page.
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pul: Test cleanup bug: in OrganizationCacheTest.testCacheIDPForLogin(), cleanup is registered with a literal "alias" instead of the created IDP alias, so the created IDPs are not removed (and cleanup may fail if no IDP with alias "alias" exists). Evidence: inside the loop creating idp-alias-0..19: `getCleanup().addCleanup(testRealm().identityProviders().get("alias")::remove);` and again after creating idp-alias-20: `getCleanup().addCleanup(testRealm().identityProviders().get("alias")::remove);`.

NullPointerException risk in InfinispanIdentityProviderStorageProvider.remove(String): it fetches `storedIdp = idpDelegate.getByAlias(alias)` and unconditionally calls `registerIDPLoginInvalidation(storedIdp)`. If alias does not exist, storedIdp can be null; `registerIDPLoginInvalidation` calls `getLoginPredicate().test(idp)`, and the predicate chain dereferences `idp.getOrganizationId()` / `idp.getConfig()`, causing NPE. Evidence: `IdentityProviderModel storedIdp = idpDelegate.getByAlias(alias); ... registerIDPLoginInvalidation(storedIdp);` and `getLoginPredicate()` includes `.and(idp -> idp.getOrganizationId() == null || Boolean.parseBoolean(idp.getConfig().get(...)))`.

NullPointerException risk in IdentityProviderStorageProvider.LoginFilter.getLoginPredicate(): the new predicate dereferences `idp.getConfig().get(...)` without checking `getConfig()` for null, so implementations returning null config will NPE when evaluating the predicate for org-linked IDPs. Evidence: added `.and(idp -> idp.getOrganizationId() == null || Boolean.parseBoolean(idp.getConfig().get(OrganizationModel.BROKER_PUBLIC)))`.

NullPointerException risk in InfinispanIdentityProviderStorageProvider.update(): `IdentityProviderModel idpById = getById(model.getInternalId());` is passed to `registerIDPLoginInvalidationOnUpdate(idpById, model)` without null-check. If getById returns null, `registerIDPLoginInvalidationOnUpdate` evaluates `getLoginPredicate().test(original)` and may call `original.getOrganizationId()`, causing NPE. Evidence: `registerIDPLoginInvalidationOnUpdate(idpById, model);` and inside method `getLoginPredicate().test(original)` plus `Objects.equals(original.getOrganizationId(), ...)`.
https://github.com/grafana/grafana/pull/: TablesList() is now wired to the new in-repo DB, but DB.RunCommands is a stub returning "not implemented", so table extraction will deterministically fail at runtime. Evidence: pkg/expr/sql/parser.go uses duckDB := NewInMemoryDB(); ret, err := duckDB.RunCommands(...). pkg/expr/sql/db.go: func (db *DB) RunCommands(...) (string, error) { return "", errors.New("not implemented") }

SQLCommand.Execute will always fail at runtime because it now uses sql.NewInMemoryDB() and calls QueryFramesInto(), which is a stub returning "not implemented". Evidence: pkg/expr/sql_command.go: db := sql.NewInMemoryDB(); err := db.QueryFramesInto(...). pkg/expr/sql/db.go: func (db *DB) QueryFramesInto(...) error { return errors.New("not implemented") }

Compilation error: pkg/expr/sql_command.go now references sql.NewInMemoryDB() but the diff shows only removal of the duck import and does not show adding an import for the local pkg/expr/sql package, so `sql` would be an unresolved identifier. Evidence: in sql_command.go imports, only `github.com/scottlepp/go-duck/duck` is removed; no `.../pkg/expr/sql` import is added while code changes to `db := sql.NewInMemoryDB()`.

Unconditional error-level logging in cleanup paths: xorm_store.go now uses r.log.Error(...) for normal control flow even when err is nil (e.g., "Annotations to clean by time", "cleaned annotations by time", "Annotations to clean by count", "cleaned annotations by count", "Tags to clean", "cleaned tags"). This will emit error logs on successful cleanup runs and can trigger false alerts. Evidence: multiple r.log.Error(...) calls include "err", err/y but are executed regardless of error state.

SQLite parameter-limit handling is incorrect: deleteByIDs decides whether to inline IDs based on r.cfg.AnnotationCleanupJobBatchSize > 999, but the actual number of SQL variables is len(ids). If len(ids) exceeds SQLite's variable limit while batch size is <= 999 (or if the limit differs by build), the code will still generate too many placeholders and fail. Evidence: const sqliteParameterLimit = 999; condition uses AnnotationCleanupJobBatchSize, but placeholders are built from len(ids): strings.Repeat(",?", len(ids)-1).

cleanup_test.go likely will not compile on Go < 1.21: createTestAnnotations uses min(...) in slice expressions (newAnnotations[i:min(...)] and newAnnotationTags[i:min(...)]), but no min helper is defined/imported in the diff. Evidence: sess.InsertMulti(newAnnotations[i:min(i+batchsize, len(newAnnotations))]) and same for tags.

cleanup.go leaks ticker resources: CleanUpService.Run creates a time.NewTicker(...) and never calls ticker.Stop(). If Run returns (e.g., on ctx cancellation), the ticker is not stopped. Evidence: ticker := time.NewTicker(time.Minute * 1) followed by for/select loop with no defer ticker.Stop().

DualWriterMode3.Create: on Storage.Create error it calls d.recordLegacyDuration(true, mode3Str, options.Kind, method, startStorage) instead of recording storage duration (misattributed metrics). Evidence: in Create(), after `created, err := d.Storage.Create(...)`, the error branch uses `recordLegacyDuration` with `startStorage`.

DualWriterMode3.Delete: builds an enriched logger `log := d.Log.WithValues("name", name, "kind", options.Kind, "method", method)` but installs `d.Log` into the context (`ctx = klog.NewContext(ctx, d.Log)`), dropping per-call fields. Evidence: Delete() sets context with `d.Log` not `log`.

DualWriterMode3.Delete: records storage duration with `name` where other calls use `options.Kind` (label/value mixup). Evidence: `d.recordStorageDuration(false, mode3Str, name, method, startStorage)`.

DualWriterMode3.Update: on Storage.Update error it calls d.recordLegacyDuration(true, mode3Str, options.Kind, method, startStorage) instead of recording storage duration (misattributed metrics). Evidence: Update() error branch after `res, async, err := d.Storage.Update(...)` uses `recordLegacyDuration`.

DualWriterMode3.DeleteCollection: legacy async path records storage duration instead of legacy duration. Evidence: goroutine after `d.Legacy.DeleteCollection(...)` calls `d.recordStorageDuration(err != nil, mode3Str, options.Kind, method, startLegacy)`.

dualwriter_mode3_test.go: multiple tests call NewDualWriter(..., p) but `p` is not defined in those test scopes (compile error). Evidence: TestMode3_Create/List/Delete/DeleteCollection/Update each contain `dw := NewDualWriter(Mode3, ls, us, p)` without a local `p := prometheus.NewRegistry()` (only TestMode3_Get defines `p`).

dualwriter_mode1_test.go: removed local `p := prometheus.NewRegistry()` but still passes `p` to NewDualWriter (compile error if no outer-scope `p`). Evidence: in TestMode1_Get diff shows deletion of `p := prometheus.NewRegistry()` while keeping `dw := NewDualWriter(Mode1, ls, us, p)`.

GrafanaGroupLoader can throw at runtime when the Prometheus response contains no groups: it calls `.map` on a possibly-undefined value. In `GrafanaGroupLoader.tsx` the render uses `promResponse.data.groups.at(0)?.rules.map(...)`; optional chaining only guards `rules`, but `.map` is still invoked on `undefined` if `groups.at(0)` is undefined or has no `rules`.

useAbilities.ts introduces `useAllGrafanaPromRuleAbilities` which calls `useGrafanaRulesSilenceSupport()` but that symbol is not imported or defined anywhere in the diff. This is a concrete compile-time/runtime error (unresolved identifier) in `public/app/features/alerting/unified/hooks/useAbilities.ts`.

useAbilities.ts introduces `useIsGrafanaPromRuleEditable` which references `ctx.hasPermissionInMetadata(...)`, but `ctx` is not defined/imported in this file. This is a concrete compile-time error (unresolved identifier) in `public/app/features/alerting/unified/hooks/useAbilities.ts`.

RuleActionsButtons.V2 calls `useRulerRuleAbility(rule, ...)` where `rule` is now optional (Props allow only `promRule`). The hook signature in the same diff still expects `rule: RulerRuleDTO` (non-optional). This is a concrete type/contract violation that will fail TypeScript compilation or crash if the hook dereferences `rule`.

Frontend/backend contract mismatch for anonymousDeviceLimit: TS declares `anonymousDeviceLimit: number | undefined` (packages/grafana-data/src/types/config.ts) and runtime boot config defaults it to `undefined` (packages/grafana-runtime/src/config.ts), but the backend DTO uses a non-pointer `int64` (pkg/api/dtos/frontend_settings.go) and always sets it from cfg (pkg/api/frontendsettings.go), with cfg defaulting to 0 when unset (pkg/setting/setting.go). This makes `undefined` impossible over the wire and breaks the declared optional semantics.

`updateDevice` misclassifies failures as `ErrDeviceLimitReached`: it returns `ErrDeviceLimitReached` whenever the UPDATE affects 0 rows (pkg/services/anonymous/anonimpl/anonstore/database.go), but 0 rows can occur because the row doesn't exist or because the `updated_at BETWEEN ? AND ?` predicate doesn't match. This can incorrectly report "device limit reached" for other conditions.

`updateDevice` matches on `updated_at BETWEEN` using a range derived from the incoming `device.UpdatedAt` (pkg/services/anonymous/anonimpl/anonstore/database.go). If the caller-provided `UpdatedAt` differs from the stored row's timestamp, the UPDATE won't match and will be treated as `ErrDeviceLimitReached`, making correctness depend on caller timestamps.

Behavioral change: anonymous authentication can now fail when device limit is reached. `Anonymous.Authenticate` now calls `TagDevice` synchronously and returns an error if it is `anonstore.ErrDeviceLimitReached` (pkg/services/anonymous/anonimpl/client.go). Previously tagging was async/best-effort and did not block authentication. This is a concrete production behavior change that can deny anonymous access when the limit is hit.
https://github.com/ai-code-review-evalua: Interface mismatch: `FallbackLocaleList#ensure_loaded!` calls `I18n.ensure_loaded!`, but the diff only adds `ensure_loaded!` as an instance method inside `lib/freedom_patches/translate_accelerator.rb` (on the accelerator/backend), not as a module-level `I18n.ensure_loaded!` API. Evidence: `config/initializers/i18n.rb`: `self[I18n.locale].each { |l| I18n.ensure_loaded! l }` vs `lib/freedom_patches/translate_accelerator.rb`: `def ensure_loaded!(locale) ... end` (added there). This will raise `NoMethodError` unless `I18n.ensure_loaded!` is defined elsewhere (not shown in this diff).

`ApplicationController#set_locale` unconditionally calls `I18n.fallbacks.ensure_loaded!`, but `ensure_loaded!` is only defined on the custom `FallbackLocaleList` assigned in the new initializer. If that initializer hasn’t run (or `I18n.fallbacks` is not this class), this call will raise `NoMethodError`. Evidence: `app/controllers/application_controller.rb`: `I18n.fallbacks.ensure_loaded!` and `config/initializers/i18n.rb`: defines `FallbackLocaleList#ensure_loaded!` and assigns `I18n.fallbacks = FallbackLocaleList.new`.
https://github.com/grafana/grafana/pull/: TestLogger.FromContext returns a brand-new logger instance, so logs emitted via logger.FromContext(ctx).Info/... will be recorded on the new TestLogger rather than the original instance the test likely holds. Evidence: pkg/plugins/log/fake.go adds `func (f *TestLogger) FromContext(_ context.Context) Logger { return NewTestLogger() }`, while LoggerMiddleware now uses `m.logger.FromContext(ctx).Info(...)` (pkg/services/pluginsintegration/clientmiddleware/logger_middleware.go). This breaks expected log capture/call counts in tests using TestLogger.

MetricsMiddleware is incorrectly constructed as a shared, stateful instance whose `next` field is mutated on each wrap, which can cross-wire downstream clients if the same middleware is applied more than once. Evidence: pkg/services/pluginsintegration/clientmiddleware/metrics_middleware.go: `imw := newMetricsMiddleware(...)` then `return plugins.ClientMiddlewareFunc(func(next plugins.Client) plugins.Client { imw.next = next; return imw })`. This reuses the same `*MetricsMiddleware` for all wraps instead of creating a new instance per `next`.
https://github.com/ai-code-review-evalua: Migration can crash when expected site_settings rows are missing: it indexes `[0]` on the result of `execute(...)` without checking any rows exist. In `db/migrate/20150818190757_create_embeddable_hosts.rb`, `execute(...)[0]['id']` and `execute(...)[0]['value']` will raise if the query returns 0 rows (e.g., no `embed_category` row or no `uncategorized_category_id` row).

Migration inserts host values via raw string interpolation without escaping/quoting, which will break on hosts containing a single quote and can corrupt the migration. In `db/migrate/20150818190757_create_embeddable_hosts.rb`: `execute "INSERT INTO embeddable_hosts ... VALUES ('#{h}', #{category_id}, ...)"`.

EmbeddableHost `before_validation` can raise NoMethodError when `host` is nil because it calls `sub!` unconditionally. In `app/models/embeddable_host.rb`: `self.host.sub!(...)` and `self.host.sub!(...)` will crash if `self.host` is nil (e.g., new record or malformed params).

EmbeddableHost host allowlist lookup is case-sensitive due to lowercasing only the DB side. In `app/models/embeddable_host.rb`, `record_for_host` does `host = uri.host` then `where("lower(host) = ?", host).first` without downcasing `host`, so mixed-case referer hosts (or stored hosts) can fail to match.

Admin::EmbeddableHostsController#update/#destroy do not handle missing records and will 500 on nil dereference. In `app/controllers/admin/embeddable_hosts_controller.rb`, `host = EmbeddableHost.where(id: params[:id]).first` followed by `save_host(host)` (which does `host.host = ...`) and `host.destroy` will raise if `host` is nil.

Admin::EmbeddableHostsController#save_host can crash on missing params because it assumes `params[:embeddable_host]` exists. In `app/controllers/admin/embeddable_hosts_controller.rb`: `params[:embeddable_host][:host]` and `params[:embeddable_host][:category_id]` will raise if `params[:embeddable_host]` is nil.

Ember admin route likely requests the wrong endpoint: `admin/routes/admin-embedding.js.es6` uses `this.store.find('embedding')` (no id), but the backend exposes only `GET /admin/customize/embedding` (singular) and the adapter path is `/admin/customize/embedding`. Without an id, `store.find` typically generates a `/:id` URL, so this is likely to 404/misroute.

desktop/topic-post.scss: Light-theme value regression in replies author link. The color changed from `scale-color($primary, $lightness: 30%)` to `dark-light-choose(scale-color($primary, $lightness: 70%), scale-color($secondary, $lightness: 30%))`, which alters the light-theme branch from 30% to 70% (unrelated to the stated goal of only adjusting dark theme behavior). Evidence: `@@ -288,10 +288,10 @@ nav.post-controls { ... a { font-weight: bold; - color: scale-color($primary, $lightness: 30%); + color: dark-light-choose(scale-color($primary, $lightness: 70%), scale-color($secondary, $lightness: 30%)); } }`

mobile/modal.scss: Light-theme value regression for `.custom-message-length`. The color changed from `scale-color($primary, $lightness: 70%)` to `dark-light-choose(scale-color($primary, $lightness: 30%), scale-color($secondary, $lightness: 70%))`, changing the light-theme branch from 70% to 30%. Evidence: `@@ -99,7 +99,7 @@ .custom-message-length { - color: scale-color($primary, $lightness: 70%); + color: dark-light-choose(scale-color($primary, $lightness: 30%), scale-color($secondary, $lightness: 70%)); }`

mobile/topic-post.scss: Light-theme value regression for `a.star h3`. The color changed from `scale-color($primary, $lightness: 20%)` to `dark-light-choose(scale-color($primary, $lightness: 50%), scale-color($secondary, $lightness: 50%))`, changing the light-theme branch from 20% to 50%. Evidence: `@@ -179,7 +179,7 @@ a.star { h3 { ... - color: scale-color($primary, $lightness: 20%); + color: dark-light-choose(scale-color($primary, $lightness: 50%), scale-color($secondary, $lightness: 50%)); } }`

desktop/user.scss: Light-theme value regression for `.user-profile-names .name`. The color changed from `scale-color($primary, $lightness: 30%)` to `dark-light-choose(scale-color($primary, $lightness: 50%), scale-color($secondary, $lightness: 50%))`, changing the light-theme branch from 30% to 50% and making it inconsistent with the base user styling. Evidence: `@@ -519,7 +519,7 @@ .user-profile-names { .name { ... - color: scale-color($primary, $lightness: 30%); + color: dark-light-choose(scale-color($primary, $lightness: 50%), scale-color($secondary, $lightness: 50%)); } }`

mobile/user.scss: Light-theme value regression for `.user-profile-names .name`. The color changed from `scale-color($primary, $lightness: 30%)` to `dark-light-choose(scale-color($primary, $lightness: 50%), scale-color($secondary, $lightness: 50%))`, changing the light-theme branch from 30% to 50% and making it inconsistent with the base user styling. Evidence: `@@ -494,13 +494,13 @@ .user-profile-names { .name { ... - color: scale-color($primary, $lightness: 30%); + color: dark-light-choose(scale-color($primary, $lightness: 50%), scale-color($secondary, $lightness: 50%)); } }`

Pagination totalPages is off-by-one when user_count is an exact multiple of limit. In admin-group controller: `totalPages: function() { if (this.get("user_count") == 0) { return 0; } return Math.floor(this.get("user_count") / this.get("limit")) + 1; }` (e.g. 100/50 => 3 instead of 2). Should use ceil or equivalent.

Admin member removal/add routes mismatch: client calls `/admin/groups/:id/members.json` but controller actions require `:group_id`. JS: `Discourse.ajax('/admin/groups/' + this.get('id') + '/members.json', ...)`; controller: `group = Group.find(params.require(:group_id).to_i)` in both `add_members` and `remove_member`. This will raise on `params.require(:group_id)`.

Admin group template references `members` but controller/route no longer sets a `members` property on the controller; `findMembers()` sets `members` on the Group model. Template: `{{each member in members itemView="group-member"}}`; route now only does `controller.set("model", model); model.findMembers();`. This will render nothing/undefined unless `members` is aliased elsewhere (not shown in diff).

Group members page template now iterates `members` but the route sets `controller.model` to the Group and does not set a `members` property on the controller. Template: `{{#each m in members}}`; route: `controller.set("model", model); model.findMembers();`. As written, `members` will be undefined unless provided elsewhere.

New admin group-member view uses an incorrect templateName that likely won’t resolve. View sets `templateName: "admin/templates/group_member"` while the template file is `app/assets/javascripts/admin/templates/group_member.hbs` (logical name would typically be `admin/group_member` or `group_member`, not including `templates`). This can prevent the item view from rendering.

Discourse.Group#findMembers now returns `undefined` when name is empty (`if (Em.isEmpty(this.get('name'))) { return ; }`), whereas it previously returned a resolved promise. Any caller that expects a promise and chains `.then(...)` can now throw at runtime.

Client shows the wrong error message for blocked emails: when an email is in `rejectedEmails`, the client returns a failed validation with `reason: I18n.t('user.email.invalid')` instead of using the new server-side blocked message (`user.email.blocked`). Evidence: create_account_controller.js rejectedEmails branch uses `I18n.t('user.email.invalid')`, while server adds `user.email.blocked` in EmailValidator and locale.

Email domain restriction regex is not anchored/boundary-checked, so it can match unintended domains/substrings (e.g., blacklist/whitelist entry `evil.com` can match `user@not-evil.com`, and `example.com` can match `example.com.evil`). Evidence: lib/validators/email_validator.rb builds `Regexp.new("@(#{domains})", true)` and tests `value =~ regexp` with no end/segment boundaries.

Email domain restriction regex does not properly escape regex metacharacters in the SiteSetting value (only `.` is escaped), so settings containing characters like `|`, `(`, `)`, `*`, `+`, etc. will change regex meaning and cause incorrect allow/deny behavior. Evidence: lib/validators/email_validator.rb: `domains = setting.gsub('.', '\\.')` then `Regexp.new("@(#{domains})", true)`.

BlockedEmail.should_block? has a real lost-update race on `match_count`/`last_match_at` because it performs a read-modify-write in Ruby (`match_count += 1` then `save`) without atomic update/locking, so concurrent calls can undercount and overwrite timestamps. Evidence: app/models/blocked_email.rb `record.match_count += 1; record.last_match_at = Time.zone.now; record.save`.
Missing trailing newline at end of lib/validators/email_validator.rb. Evidence: diff explicitly shows `\ No newline at end of file`.

Legacy flexbox `order` polyfill is incorrect: `-webkit-box-ordinal-group` / `-moz-box-ordinal-group` are 1-based, but the mixin passes `$val` directly. In `@mixin order($val) { -webkit-box-ordinal-group: $val; -moz-box-ordinal-group: $val; ... }` and usage `.panel { @include order(3); }`, older `-webkit-box`/`-moz-box` implementations will interpret this as ordinal-group 3 (equivalent to modern `order: 2`), causing inconsistent ordering across browsers.

Invalid vendor-prefixed property in `align-items` mixin: `-ms-align-items` is not a real CSS property. The diff adds `@mixin align-items($alignment) { ... -ms-flex-align: $alignment; -ms-align-items: $alignment; ... }`, where `-ms-align-items` will be ignored (indicating a broken/incorrect cross-browser rule).

Invalid legacy value for old WebKit box model: `@include align-items(baseline)` (used in `badges.css.scss` for `&.bullet`) expands to `-webkit-box-align: baseline;`, but `-webkit-box-align` (old `-webkit-box` flexbox) does not support `baseline`, so baseline alignment will not work in those legacy implementations.

Serializer can raise URI::InvalidURIError because only the first URI() call is rescued. In `website_name`, `website_host = URI(website.to_s).host rescue nil` is protected, but later branches call `URI(website.to_s).path` without rescue/guard. If `website` is an invalid URI string, this will raise and break serialization (500). Evidence: `website_host = URI(website.to_s).host rescue nil` followed by `website_host + URI(website.to_s).path` in multiple branches.

Potential NoMethodError if `Discourse.current_hostname` is nil/blank. `website_name` unconditionally calls `discourse_host.split('.')` and `discourse_host.ends_with?`, which will crash when `Discourse.current_hostname` returns nil. Evidence: `discourse_host = Discourse.current_hostname` then `discourse_host.split('.')` and `discourse_host.ends_with?` with no nil guard.

Cook method default is set to raw_html due to enum/migration mismatch: Post.cook_methods is Enum.new(:regular, :raw_html) (so :regular=>0, :raw_html=>1), but migration adds posts.cook_method with default: 1, null: false. This makes all new posts default to :raw_html, causing Post#cook to return raw (skip cooking pipeline) for normal posts. Evidence: db/migrate/20131219203905_add_cook_method_to_posts.rb (default: 1) + app/models/post.rb (Enum.new(:regular, :raw_html) and `return raw if cook_method == Post.cook_methods[:raw_html]`).

EmbedController spec is inconsistent with controller behavior: controller enqueues Jobs.enqueue(:retrieve_topic, ...) when no TopicEmbed exists, but spec expects TopicRetriever.new(...).retrieve to be called. This will fail. Evidence: app/controllers/embed_controller.rb enqueues job; spec/controllers/embed_controller_spec.rb expects TopicRetriever to be instantiated and `retrieve` called.

embed layout uses an invalid postMessage targetOrigin: it passes `request.referer` (a full URL including path/query) as the targetOrigin argument to parent.postMessage, but postMessage requires an origin (scheme + host + optional port) or '*'. This will cause resize messaging to fail. Evidence: app/views/layouts/embed.html.erb: `parent.postMessage(..., '<%= request.referer %>');`

PollFeed can crash when RSS items have no content: it calls `i.content.scrub` without guarding for nil, so nil content raises NoMethodError. Evidence: app/jobs/scheduled/poll_feed.rb: `content = CGI.unescapeHTML(i.content.scrub)`.

Client-side upload size validation is now hard-coded to 10MB, ignoring per-type SiteSettings. In `app/assets/javascripts/discourse/lib/utilities.js` the code changed from `var maxSizeKB = Discourse.SiteSettings['max_' + type + '_size_kb'];` to `var maxSizeKB = 10 * 1024; // 10MB`, so the client will enforce/show a limit that may not match configured `max_*_size_kb` values.

The 413 (entity too large) handler now hard-codes the max size to 10MB, ignoring `Discourse.SiteSettings.max_image_size_kb`. In `utilities.js` it changed from `var maxSizeKB = Discourse.SiteSettings.max_image_size_kb;` to `var maxSizeKB = 10 * 1024; // 10 MB`, which can display an incorrect limit in the error message when the site setting differs.

API URL download limit is hard-coded to 10MB instead of respecting `SiteSetting.max_image_size_kb`. In `app/controllers/uploads_controller.rb` it changed from `FileHelper.download(url, SiteSetting.max_image_size_kb.kilobytes, ...)` to `FileHelper.download(url, 10.megabytes, ...)`, so admin configuration no longer controls this path.

UploadsController calls `OptimizedImage.downsize` with an invalid 3rd argument: it passes a quality string (`"80%"`) where the updated `OptimizedImage.downsize(from, to, dimensions, opts={})` expects an ImageMagick geometry/dimensions string (e.g. `"800x600"`). Evidence: `OptimizedImage.downsize(tempfile.path, tempfile.path, "80%", allow_animation: ...)` in `uploads_controller.rb`, and `def self.downsize(from, to, dimensions, opts={})` passing `dimensions` through to `*_instructions` in `optimized_image.rb`. This will generate invalid convert instructions and prevent downsizing from working.

Breaking API change in `OptimizedImage`: `downsize` is defined twice with different signatures, and the later definition overwrites the earlier one. In `app/models/optimized_image.rb` there is still a `def self.downsize(from, to, max_width, max_height, opts={})` (now calling optimize with `"#{max_width}x#{max_height}"`), followed by a new `def self.downsize(from, to, dimensions, opts={})`. The second definition replaces the first at runtime, so any existing callers using the 4-arg width/height API will raise `ArgumentError`.

Email template renders an invalid CSS class name: `app/views/email/notification.html.erb` uses `<h4 class='.previous-discussion'>...`, so the class attribute literally contains a leading dot and selectors expecting `previous-discussion` will not match.

Email unsubscribe translation now requires `%{unsubscribe_url}`, but MessageBuilder will raise if `add_unsubscribe_link: true` is used without providing `unsubscribe_url`: `config/locales/server.en.yml` adds `(%{unsubscribe_url})` and `lib/email/message_builder.rb` calls `I18n.t('unsubscribe_link', template_args)` with no default for `unsubscribe_url`.
https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/pull/2: CredentialActionsDropdown hard-codes date formatting locale to "en-US", ignoring the active UI locale from useLocale(), so non-en-US users will see incorrectly localized timestamps. Evidence: `new Intl.DateTimeFormat("en-US", { dateStyle: "short", timeStyle: "short" }).format(...)` in packages/features/apps/components/CredentialActionsDropdown.tsx.

CredentialActionsDropdown assumes `cacheUpdatedAt` is a Date at runtime, but it comes from tRPC/JSON and will typically be serialized (e.g., string). `new Date(cacheUpdatedAt)` can produce an invalid date and `Intl.DateTimeFormat().format(...)` will throw `RangeError: Invalid time value`. Evidence: prop type `cacheUpdatedAt?: Date | null` and usage `.format(new Date(cacheUpdatedAt))` in packages/features/apps/components/CredentialActionsDropdown.tsx; server sets `cacheUpdatedAt` from Prisma Date in connectedCalendars.handler.ts.

GoogleCalendarService calls `SelectedCalendarRepository.updateManyByCredentialId(this.credential.id, {})` with an empty update `data` object. Prisma `updateMany` requires at least one field to update; passing `{}` is invalid and will throw at runtime, and it also does not accomplish the stated goal of updating `updatedAt`. Evidence: packages/app-store/googlecalendar/lib/CalendarService.ts and `updateManyByCredentialId(..., data)` implementation in packages/lib/server/repository/selectedCalendar.ts.

scripts/test-gcal-webhooks.sh uses macOS/BSD-specific `sed -i ''` syntax, which fails on GNU sed (common on Linux), making the script non-portable and likely to error in many environments. Evidence: `sed -i '' -E ...` in scripts/test-gcal-webhooks.sh.

deleteCacheHandler throws a plain `Error` instead of a `TRPCError`, which in tRPC typically surfaces as `INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR` rather than a proper NOT_FOUND/FORBIDDEN, breaking expected API error semantics. Evidence: `throw new Error("Credential not found or access denied")` in packages/trpc/server/routers/viewer/calendars/deleteCache.handler.ts.
https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/pull/8: Incorrect Dayjs equality check in date override logic: `if (dayjs(date.start).add(utcOffset, "minutes") === dayjs(date.end).add(utcOffset, "minutes"))` compares two different Dayjs object instances by reference, so it will never be true. This breaks the intended `start == end` override branch in `checkIfIsAvailable` (packages/trpc/server/routers/viewer/slots.ts).

Copy/paste bug in working-hours bounds check: `end` is computed from `slotStartTime` instead of the slot end time, making the end-of-slot validation ineffective. Evidence in `checkIfIsAvailable`: `const start = slotStartTime.hour() * 60 + slotStartTime.minute(); const end = slotStartTime.hour() * 60 + slotStartTime.minute();` (packages/trpc/server/routers/viewer/slots.ts).

Type contract mismatch: `checkIfIsAvailable` declares `dateOverrides?: { start: Date; end: Date; }[]`, but `getSchedule` now builds `dateOverrides` entries that include extra fields (`userId`, `timeZone`). Evidence: `availability.dateOverrides.map((override) => ({ userId: ..., timeZone: ..., ...override }))` (packages/trpc/server/routers/viewer/slots.ts).

Invalid type import/export: `slots.ts` imports `WorkingHours` from `@calcom/types/schedule`, but the shown `packages/types/schedule.d.ts` does not export `WorkingHours` (it only exports `TimeRange` and `Schedule`). This will fail type-check/compilation.
https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/pull/1: DeleteMany cleanup now deletes reminders of any method when retryCount > 1 because the new OR branch is not scoped to method=SMS. Evidence: prisma.workflowReminder.deleteMany({ where: { OR: [ { method: WorkflowMethods.SMS, scheduledDate: { lte: dayjs().toISOString() } }, { retryCount: { gt: 1 } } ] } }); This can unintentionally delete non-SMS reminders (e.g., email) once their retryCount exceeds 1.

retryCount increment uses a stale in-memory value (reminder.retryCount + 1) instead of an atomic increment, which can lose updates under concurrent runs. Evidence: prisma.workflowReminder.update({ where: { id: reminder.id }, data: { retryCount: reminder.retryCount + 1 } }) in both the non-success branch and catch; reminder.retryCount comes from an earlier findMany result.
https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/pull/2: Type contract/compilation bug: constructor now accepts `InsightsBookingServicePublicOptions` but `this.options` is typed as `InsightsBookingServiceOptions | null` and is assigned directly (`this.options = options;`). This is not assignable because the zod-derived discriminated union requires `teamId` when `scope: "team"`, while `InsightsBookingServicePublicOptions` makes `teamId` optional. Evidence: `private options: InsightsBookingServiceOptions | null;` + constructor param `options: InsightsBookingServicePublicOptions;` + assignment `this.options = options;`.

Type mismatch in internal method calls: `buildOrgAuthorizationCondition`/`buildTeamAuthorizationCondition` parameters are typed as `Extract<InsightsBookingServiceOptions, { scope: "org"|"team" }>` but are called with `this.options` which (per constructor change) is sourced from `InsightsBookingServicePublicOptions`. This is an incompatible type and will fail TypeScript (or require unsafe casts). Evidence: `return await this.buildOrgAuthorizationCondition(this.options);` / `return await this.buildTeamAuthorizationCondition(this.options);` with method signatures still using `Extract<InsightsBookingServiceOptions, ...>`.

Runtime correctness bug: `teamId` is optional in `InsightsBookingServicePublicOptions`, but `buildTeamAuthorizationCondition` uses it as required in SQL and repository calls. If a caller constructs the service with `{ scope: "team", ... }` but omits `teamId`, the code will generate SQL like `("teamId" = ${options.teamId})` and call `findByIdAndParentId({ id: options.teamId, ... })` with `undefined`, leading to incorrect filtering or runtime errors. Evidence: `teamId?: number;` and `Prisma.sql\`("teamId" = ${options.teamId}) AND ("isTeamBooking" = true)\`` plus `findByIdAndParentId({ id: options.teamId, ... })`.
https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/pull/1: APP_CREDENTIAL_SHARING_ENABLED is not a boolean; it evaluates to a string (the encryption key) when both env vars are set. Evidence: `export const APP_CREDENTIAL_SHARING_ENABLED = process.env.CALCOM_WEBHOOK_SECRET && process.env.CALCOM_APP_CREDENTIAL_ENCRYPTION_KEY;` (packages/lib/constants.ts).

parseRefreshTokenResponse defines an invalid Zod schema using computed keys; `[z.string().toString()]` becomes a literal key (e.g. "[object Object]") and does not implement the intended catch-all validation. Evidence: `const minimumTokenResponseSchema = z.object({ access_token: z.string(), [z.string().toString()]: z.number(), [z.string().optional().toString()]: z.unknown().optional(), });` (packages/app-store/_utils/oauth/parseRefreshTokenResponse.ts).

parseRefreshTokenResponse returns a Zod safeParse result object, but at least one caller stores it directly as the credential `key`, persisting `{success, data}` instead of the credential payload. Evidence: `const key = parseRefreshTokenResponse(googleCredentials, googleCredentialSchema); await prisma.credential.update({ ..., data: { key }, });` (packages/app-store/googlecalendar/lib/CalendarService.ts) while parseRefreshTokenResponse ends with `return refreshTokenResponse;` (packages/app-store/_utils/oauth/parseRefreshTokenResponse.ts).

GoogleCalendarService passes the wrong object into parseRefreshTokenResponse: it validates `googleCredentials` (mutated local credential object) instead of the refreshed token response (`res.data`). Evidence: `const token = res?.data; ... googleCredentials.access_token = token.access_token; ... const key = parseRefreshTokenResponse(googleCredentials, googleCredentialSchema);` (packages/app-store/googlecalendar/lib/CalendarService.ts).

refreshOAuthTokens returns a raw Fetch `Response` when sync is enabled, but some call sites expect provider-specific objects (not a Response), causing runtime shape mismatches. Evidence: refreshOAuthTokens sync branch `return response;` where `response` is from `fetch(...)` (packages/app-store/_utils/oauth/refreshOAuthTokens.ts); Hubspot assigns it to `HubspotToken`: `const hubspotRefreshToken: HubspotToken = await refreshOAuthTokens(...);` (packages/app-store/hubspot/lib/CalendarService.ts); Zoho Bigin later uses `.data`: `if (!tokenInfo.data.error) { ... }` after `const tokenInfo = await refreshOAuthTokens(...)` (packages/app-store/zoho-bigin/lib/CalendarService.ts).

Zoho Bigin CalendarService passes `credentialId` as the `userId` argument to refreshOAuthTokens, but refreshOAuthTokens uses that value as `calcomUserId` in the sync payload. This sends the wrong identifier when sync is enabled. Evidence: `refreshOAuthTokens(..., "zoho-bigin", credentialId)` (packages/app-store/zoho-bigin/lib/CalendarService.ts) and `body: new URLSearchParams({ calcomUserId: userId.toString(), appSlug })` (packages/app-store/_utils/oauth/refreshOAuthTokens.ts).

SalesforceCalendarService misuses parseRefreshTokenResponse return type: it treats the return as a safeParse result with `.success`/`.data`, but parseRefreshTokenResponse throws on failure and returns the safeParse object on success, making the `.success` check inconsistent/dead and indicating a contract mismatch. Evidence: `const accessTokenParsed = parseRefreshTokenResponse(...); if (!accessTokenParsed.success) { ... } ... { ...accessTokenParsed.data, ... }` (packages/app-store/salesforce/lib/CalendarService.ts) vs parseRefreshTokenResponse: `if (!refreshTokenResponse.success) { throw new Error(...); } return refreshTokenResponse;` (packages/app-store/_utils/oauth/parseRefreshTokenResponse.ts).
https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/pull/7: Email reminder cancellation no longer cancels the provider send in the common path: call sites now invoke `deleteScheduledEmailReminder(reminder.id, reminder.referenceId)` without `immediateDelete`, and the implementation only sets `cancelled: true` in DB (does not call the provider cancel API) when `referenceId` exists and `immediateDelete` is falsy. This can result in emails still being sent even though the reminder is marked cancelled. Evidence: `emailReminderManager.ts` -> `if (immediateDelete) { ... cancel ...; return; } await prisma.workflowReminder.update({ data: { cancelled: true } })`; call sites in `handleCancelBooking.ts`, `viewer/bookings.tsx`, `viewer/workflows.tsx` use the 2-arg form.

`scheduleEmailReminders.ts` cancellation query/loop does not restrict to email reminders and does not guard `referenceId` being null, yet it always calls the email provider cancel endpoint with `batch_id: reminder.referenceId`. This can attempt to cancel non-email reminders and/or send `batch_id: null` to the provider API. Evidence: `findMany({ where: { cancelled: true, scheduledDate: { lte: ... }}})` (no `method` filter) and `body: { batch_id: reminder.referenceId, status: "cancel" }` (no null check).

Multiple async reminder cancellations are fired without awaiting, so failures can be dropped and the request can complete before cancellations/DB updates finish. Evidence: `handleCancelBooking.ts` uses `forEach` and calls `deleteScheduledEmailReminder(...)` / `deleteScheduledSMSReminder(...)` without `await`; `viewer/bookings.tsx` similarly; `viewer/workflows.tsx` has `scheduledReminders.forEach(...)` without awaiting.

`viewer/workflows.tsx` uses `remindersToUpdate.forEach(async (reminder) => { ... })` but does not await the resulting promises, guaranteeing the mutation does not wait for the async work and risking unhandled rejections. Evidence: the `forEach(async ...)` block around line ~586 in the diff.

`deleteScheduledEmailReminder(..., immediateDelete=true)` cancels the external scheduled send and then returns without deleting or marking the `WorkflowReminder` row, leaving orphaned DB records. Evidence: `emailReminderManager.ts` -> `if (immediateDelete) { await client.request(...cancel...); return; }` (no DB update/delete in that branch).
https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/pull/1: Email notification logic is inverted in sendAddGuestsEmails: attendees whose email is in newGuests are sent AttendeeScheduledEmail, while everyone else is sent AttendeeAddGuestsEmail. This is backwards for a 'guests added' notification. Evidence: packages/emails/email-manager.ts `if (newGuests.includes(attendee.email)) { return sendEmail(() => new AttendeeScheduledEmail(calendarEvent, attendee)); } else { return sendEmail(() => new AttendeeAddGuestsEmail(calendarEvent, attendee)); }`.

Team admin/owner permission check incorrectly uses AND instead of OR, requiring a user to be both team admin and team owner to pass the team-role authorization path. Evidence: packages/trpc/server/routers/viewer/bookings/addGuests.handler.ts `const isTeamAdminOrOwner = (await isTeamAdmin(...)) && (await isTeamOwner(...));`.

Case-sensitivity bug in blacklist and existing-attendee comparisons: blacklist entries are lowercased but incoming `guest` is not, and attendee email comparisons are case-sensitive, allowing blacklist bypass and duplicate attendees via casing differences. Evidence: packages/trpc/server/routers/viewer/bookings/addGuests.handler.ts blacklist uses `.map((email) => email.toLowerCase())` but filter uses `!blacklistedGuestEmails.includes(guest)` and `guest === attendee.email` without normalizing.

EnableTwoFactorModal uses `formatBackupCode` before it is defined, which will throw at runtime when `/api/auth/two-factor/totp/setup` returns 200. Evidence: in `apps/web/components/settings/EnableTwoFactorModal.tsx`, inside `handleSetup` success path it does `body.backupCodes.map(formatBackupCode)` to build the Blob, but `const formatBackupCode = (code: string) => ...` is declared later in the component (TDZ for `const`).

GoogleCalendarService.updateEvent/deleteEvent fallback calendar selection is broken. In updateEvent: `const selectedCalendar = externalCalendarId ? externalCalendarId : event.destinationCalendar?.find((cal) => cal.externalId === externalCalendarId)?.externalId;` and in deleteEvent: `const calendarId = externalCalendarId ? externalCalendarId : event.destinationCalendar?.find((cal) => cal.externalId === externalCalendarId)?.externalId;`. When `externalCalendarId` is falsy, the predicate compares `cal.externalId` to `undefined`, so it will almost always return `undefined` even if `destinationCalendar` has entries, causing updates/deletes to target the wrong calendar (update uses `calendarId: selectedCalendar` without a default; delete falls back to `defaultCalendarId`).

EventManager.create() can crash when destinationCalendar is missing/empty. Code destructures `const [mainHostDestinationCalendar] = evt.destinationCalendar ?? [];` and then dereferences without optional chaining: `if (evt.location === MeetLocationType && mainHostDestinationCalendar.integration !== "google_calendar") { ... }`. If `evt.destinationCalendar` is `null`/`[]`, `mainHostDestinationCalendar` is `undefined` and `.integration` throws.

EventManager.updateAllCalendarEvents can call `updateEvent` with an undefined credential. In the `reference.credentialId` branch it does a lookup in `this.calendarCredentials`, then a DB fallback; if both fail, it still executes `result.push(updateEvent(credential, event, bookingRefUid, calenderExternalId));` where `credential` can be `undefined`, leading to runtime failure inside `updateEvent/getCalendar`.

Calendar interface signature change is not applied to all implementations in this diff. `packages/types/Calendar.d.ts` now requires `createEvent(event: CalendarEvent, credentialId: number)`, and `CalendarManager` calls `calendar.createEvent(calEvent, credential.id)`, but `packages/app-store/larkcalendar/lib/CalendarService.ts` still defines `async createEvent(event: CalendarEvent)` and `packages/app-store/office365calendar/lib/CalendarService.ts` still defines `async createEvent(event: CalendarEvent)`. This is a concrete compile/runtime contract break.
https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/pull/8: Unawaited async work introduced via `forEach(async ...)` in multiple reschedule/cancel flows. `Array.forEach` does not await async callbacks, so deletions/updates can run after the surrounding function continues/returns and errors won’t be caught by the surrounding `try/catch`. Evidence in diff: `packages/app-store/vital/lib/reschedule.ts` changed to `bookingRefsFiltered.forEach(async (bookingRef) => { ... const calendar = await getCalendar(...); return calendar?.deleteEvent(...) ... })`; same pattern in `packages/app-store/wipemycalother/lib/reschedule.ts`, `packages/trpc/server/routers/viewer/bookings.tsx`, and `packages/features/bookings/lib/handleCancelBooking.ts` recurring loop (`.forEach(async (credential) => { const calendar = await getCalendar(credential); ... await calendar?.updateEvent(...) })`).

`getCalendarCredentials` now stores `calendar` as a Promise because `getCalendar` became async, but it still assigns `const calendar = getCalendar(credential);` without awaiting. This changes the returned item shape and can break any consumer expecting `calendar` to be `Calendar | null` (not `Promise<Calendar|null>`). Evidence in diff: `packages/core/CalendarManager.ts` still does `const calendar = getCalendar(credential); return ... { ..., calendar }`, while later code had to change to `const calendar = await item.calendar;` in `getConnectedCalendars`.
